title: Who the World Forgot

sections.label: Lyrics
lines: They say the meek will never rise
But heaven knows the quiet fight
A whisper holds more truth than pride
The stars still shine when no one's looking
The broken soul, the weeping eye
They see what power fails to find
For in the dark, the flames ignite
A love that will not die
Not the crowned or the loud
But the ones who bow
To mercy, grace, and light
Blessed are the ones the world forgets
Those who love with nothing left
Heaven's near, though earth may wound
The crown is found in solitude
Rejoice and sing, your trials tell
The gates of glory know you well
The hungry heart that longs for right
Will taste the feast beyond this life
And those who mourn, though tears still fall
Will dance where no more sorrow calls
The pure in heart, they clear the haze
And see the face that angels praise
The peacemakers will build the day
When swords to gardens lay
Blessed are the ones the world forgets
Those who love with nothing left
Heaven's near, though earth may wound
The crown is found in solitude
Rejoice and sing, your trials tell
The gates of glory know you well
Blessed are the ones the world forgets
Those who love with nothing left
oh, with nothing left
Heaven's near, though earth may wound
The crown is found in solitude
Rejoice and sing, your trials tell
The gates of glory know you well
know you well, know you well…

epigraph: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill
